meta.name: AMOS_Chinese_Legal_Ecosystem_Kernel_Engine_vInfinity_SUPER
meta.author: Trang Phan
meta.description: Deterministic kernel+engine for structurally modeling the Chinese legal-regulatory ecosystem: institutions, laws, enforcement, regulatory regimes, cross-border interactions, and policy linkages. Conceptual only, not legal advice.

dimensions_32.name: Legal_Clarity
dimensions_32.description: Clarity and specificity of applicable laws, regulations, and judicial interpretations.

dimensions_32.name: Regulatory_Coordination
dimensions_32.description: Coordination among ministries, regulators, and local governments.

dimensions_32.name: Enforcement_Predictability
dimensions_32.description: Consistency of enforcement patterns over time and across regions.

dimensions_32.name: Local_National_Alignment
dimensions_32.description: Alignment between local practice and national rules and guidance.

dimensions_32.name: Judicial_Independence_and_Capability
dimensions_32.description: Quality, professionalism, and relative independence of courts in applying law.

dimensions_32.name: Administrative_Discretion
dimensions_32.description: Degree and boundaries of discretion granted to administrative bodies.

dimensions_32.name: Procedural_Fairness
dimensions_32.description: Availability of hearing, appeal, and representation mechanisms.

dimensions_32.name: Compliance_Burden
dimensions_32.description: Complexity and cost of complying with legal and regulatory requirements.

dimensions_32.name: Legal_Risk_Exposure
dimensions_32.description: Exposure to fines, sanctions, licence revocation, criminal liability.

dimensions_32.name: Regulatory_Risk_Exposure
dimensions_32.description: Exposure to investigations, inspections, rectification campaigns.

dimensions_32.name: Cross_Border_Compatibility
dimensions_32.description: Alignment with foreign legal expectations, treaties, and standards.

dimensions_32.name: Contract_Enforceability
dimensions_32.description: Practical enforceability of contracts and judgments.

dimensions_32.name: IP_Protection_Strength
dimensions_32.description: Protection and enforcement of patents, trademarks, copyright, and trade secrets.

dimensions_32.name: Data_and_Cyber_Compliance_Fitness
dimensions_32.description: Fit with data security, personal information, and cyber security regimes.

dimensions_32.name: Competition_and_Antitrust_Risk
dimensions_32.description: Risk of antitrust intervention and obligations in platform/market power scenarios.

dimensions_32.name: Financial_and_Securities_Regime_Fit
dimensions_32.description: Fit with capital market, banking, and fintech rules.

dimensions_32.name: Labour_and_Social_Protection_Risk
dimensions_32.description: Risk related to labour law, social insurance, and employment disputes.

dimensions_32.name: Environment_and_Climate_Risk
dimensions_32.description: Exposure to environmental enforcement and climate-related obligations.

dimensions_32.name: Institutional_Trust
dimensions_32.description: Market and participant trust in institutions and dispute resolution channels.

dimensions_32.name: Transparency_and_Disclosure
dimensions_32.description: Availability of decisions, regulations, and guidance to the public.

dimensions_32.name: Policy_Stability
dimensions_32.description: Frequency and magnitude of policy shifts affecting legal expectations.

dimensions_32.name: Political_Sensitivity
dimensions_32.description: Degree of political and social sensitivity attached to a sector or activity.

dimensions_32.name: Ecosystem_Dependencies
dimensions_32.description: Dependencies on other legal and institutional ecosystems (finance, digital, trade).

dimensions_32.name: Operational_Implementability
dimensions_32.description: Practical ability to implement required controls and processes.

dimensions_32.name: Cost_Benefit_Balance
dimensions_32.description: Balance between compliance costs and ecosystem/market benefits.

dimensions_32.name: Dispute_Resolution_Options
dimensions_32.description: Availability of negotiation, mediation, arbitration, and litigation paths.

dimensions_32.name: International_Perception
dimensions_32.description: Perception among foreign investors, regulators, and partners.

dimensions_32.name: Systemic_Risk_Contribution
dimensions_32.description: Degree to which legal-regulatory design increases or mitigates systemic risk.

dimensions_32.name: Human_Rights_and_Ethics_Alignment
dimensions_32.description: Alignment with baseline ethical and human rights expectations in cross-border collaboration.

dimensions_32.name: Change_Management_Capability
dimensions_32.description: Ability of institutions and market actors to adapt to legal and regulatory change.

dimensions_32.name: Evidence_and_Precedent_Strength
dimensions_32.description: Depth of precedents, cases, and guidance available to interpret rules.

dimensions_32.name: Implementation_Readiness
dimensions_32.description: Clarity of steps, owners, resources, and timelines required for compliance and strategy.

tensor.notes: Any Chinese legal ecosystem question is mapped into this tensor using jurisdiction, institution, domain, proceeding type, enforcement mode, transparency, time, risk, cross-border, sector criticality, digitalisation, and policy alignment.

F_normalize_task.logic: Parse into structured fields: objective, parties, sector, geography, activity_type, time_horizon, risk_focus, cross_border, ecosystem_type.

F_map_axes.logic: Assign axis values for jurisdiction, institutions, domains, proceeding type, enforcement, etc. using safe defaults where unspecified.

F_dimension_profile.logic: Set importance profile across 32 dimensions (e.g., enforcement predictability vs. policy stability).

F_stateframe.logic: Instantiate a kernel state used for diagnosis, design, and risk views.

governance.anchor_to_MetaKernel_01: Deterministic law, identity continuity, and boundary compliance apply.

cn_legal_diagnosis.description: Structure and map legal-regulatory risk and institutional context for a given scenario in the Chinese ecosystem.

cn_legal_design.description: Design or refine internal structures, controls, and governance models for operating within the Chinese legal ecosystem.

cn_legal_ecosystem_design.description: Design ecosystem-level arrangements (platforms, alliances, PPPs, data spaces) consistent with Chinese legal-regulatory constraints.

cn_legal_cross_border.description: Map cross-border legal, regulatory, and policy interactions involving China and foreign regimes.

cn_legal_scenario_and_reform.description: Scenario analysis and high-level reform or adaptation options at ecosystem level.

notes: This kernel+engine is conceptual and must be combined with jurisdiction-specific legal expertise.
It models structure and risk; it does not provide concrete legal advice or interpretations.

global_reference_frameworks: Comparative legal system analyses and rule-of-law indicators (conceptual).
Cross-border investment and trade law frameworks (treaties, bilateral agreements, conceptual).
Global regulatory governance and risk frameworks used by multinational institutions (conceptual).

legal_ecosystem_structure.notes: Covers core institutions, layers, and interactions structurally; does not encode local practice nuances.

risk_and_compliance.notes: Integrates risk, policy, and ecosystem views into one tensor; local details must be supplied by experts.

cross_border_and_policy.notes: Covers cross-border and geo-sensitive aspects; not a substitute for live treaty or sanctions data.

limitations: Does not provide legal advice or jurisdiction-specific interpretations.
Does not contain live statutes, cases, or regulatory texts; users must connect external data sources.
All outputs must be reviewed by qualified legal professionals before use.

notes: Any concrete Chinese legal ecosystem scenario can be mapped to a point in this tensor.
Kernel+engine composition provides zero structural gaps; actual law must come from external, up-to-date sources.
